page: Amazon Web Services r8i.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 4 vCPUs and 32.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz. This configuration is powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with a base clock speed of 3.9 GHz, containing 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The CPU architecture is x86_64, supported by the Nitro hypervisor. The processor includes an L3 cache of 480 MB, L2 cache of 2 MB, and L1d cache of 48 KB. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1890.0 and a top-tier result in Geekbench text processing at 2742.0. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also show strong performance at 33.41 GB/sec and 13.65 GB/sec respectively. However, multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmarks show relative weaknesses. The stress-ng multi-core CPU performance of 4286.46 and Geekbench multi-core score of 4363.0 fall into the bottom 25% of tested systems. Uncached memory bandwidth read and write speeds are also in the bottom 25%, measuring 31.47 GB/sec and 17.95 GB/sec respectively.

This hardware profile presents a clear tradeoff between high single-threaded execution speed and lower multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth efficiency. While the instance features 32.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 RAM, the actual memory throughput under heavy uncached workloads is relatively low. The instance is suited for memory-intensive workloads that rely on single-core performance, such as Redis key-value stores, which achieve 7.49 million SET operations per second, and lightweight text processing. It is less suited for heavy multi-threaded compilation, image processing, or large-scale database operations where multi-core scaling and high memory bandwidth are critical.
description: Amazon Web Services r8i.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 4 vCPUs and 32.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz. Powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with a 3.9 GHz clock speed, it operates on the x86_64 architecture under the Nitro hypervisor. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on a 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks reveal strong single-core capabilities, highlighted by a Geekbench single-core score of 1890.0 and top-tier text processing performance. However, multi-core scaling and uncached memory bandwidth fall into the bottom 25% of tested systems. This resource profile makes the instance suitable for memory-intensive, single-thread-reliant workloads like Redis caching, which achieves 7.49 million SET operations per second, while presenting performance tradeoffs for highly parallelized database operations or software compilation.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8i.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 4 vCPUs, 32 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, offering strong single-core performance but lower multi-core and memory bandwidth scaling.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8i.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 4 vCPUs, 32 GB DDR5 RAM, and a dedicated 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A memory-optimized configuration pairing strong single-core processing with DDR5 memory for workloads requiring high memory-to-CPU ratios without GPU acceleration.
bullet_points: 4 vCPUs and 32.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 7 GHz
Dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz
Strong single-core performance in text processing and compression benchmarks
Lower multi-core scaling and uncached memory bandwidth performance
No local storage and 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Suited for memory-intensive, single-thread-reliant tasks like Redis caching
